name: Mincemeat Sundae Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine wafer crumbs, pecans, butter, sugar, and cinnamon. Press into the bottom and 1 1/2 inches up the side of a 10-inch greased springform pan. Freeze for 30 minutes.
2. Filling: Place ice cream in a bowl; let stand at room temperature for 10 to 15 minutes or until softened. Stir in mincemeat until evenly distributed. Pack into crumb crust. Cover and freeze overnight or up to 4 weeks.
3. Caramel Sauce: In a saucepan, bring sugar and cream to boil. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 to 4 minutes or until thickened enough to coat a spoon. Do not overcook. Stir in vanilla. Let cool for 3 minutes. (Sauce can be cooked completely and stored in an airtight container for up to 4 weeks; reheat over medium heat before serving.)
4. To serve, drizzle caramel sauce over pieces of pie.
Makes 12 servings.
Please Note: Choose a fruit-based mincemeat made without suet to layer with ice cream and caramel sauce in this festive dessert.
